hero.title: Dad's assistant.
hero.tagline: Say what you need. Neo handles the rest.
hero.lead: You speak a request out loud. Neo turns it into tracked work, drafts it with AI, routes it to a real person to check, and tells you when it's done and ready for your sign-off.

thesis.title: From your brain to done.
body: You already know what needs to happen — a proposal for the Red Cross, a fix to a website, a note to a tenant. The slow part is the doing. Neo removes the doing.

Speak the request. Neo writes a first draft, opens it for a contracted expert to review and refine, and brings it back to you to approve or send back. Nothing goes out without a human and your final yes.
The near-term goal is simple: keep your output high through shoulder surgery and recovery, without you leaving the bed.
thesis.pull: Augmented, not automated. AI drafts, a person verifies, you decide.

workflow.title: One clean loop, every time.
workflow.intro: Every request — proposal, repair, message — follows the same path. Hot items start themselves; everything else waits in a queue until you say go, or until you ask “what can I knock out in five minutes?”

steps.name: Speak
steps.desc: You say what you want in plain language. Neo captures it as a tracked ticket, prompts and all.

steps.name: Sort
steps.desc: Neo tags it: which area, how urgent, how long it'll take. Hot items start now; the rest queue for later.

steps.name: Draft
steps.desc: Claude picks the right skill, drafts the work into a shared file, and opens it for review — all the technical plumbing stays hidden.

steps.name: Review
steps.desc: A contracted expert checks the draft, comments line by line, and asks for changes until it's right. Then they sign off.

steps.name: Approve
steps.desc: It comes to you. Approve it and it's done. Send it back and it returns to the expert for another pass.

workflow.loopNote: If you reject, the work loops back to “in progress” — the same way a control system re-runs its loop until the target is met.

architecture.title: Built like flight software.
architecture.intro: Mariah builds guidance, navigation and control software for satellites at Umbra. Neo borrows that discipline: one main loop that orchestrates, and self-contained modules that only run when you've turned them on.

pillars.label: The Neo loop
pillars.desc: The brain. It listens, decides which area a request belongs to, loads the right skill, and tracks progress. It holds no sensitive data itself.

pillars.label: Modules
pillars.desc: Each area of life is its own piece, loaded only when you need it. Lighter, faster, and each one can be improved on its own.

pillars.label: Private data layer
pillars.desc: Your templates, past proposals, pricing and client details live here — never shared, never published. Neo reads from it but never exposes it.

pillars.label: Skill loader
pillars.desc: Skills like “nonprofit proposal” or “government contract” live in your private layer. Neo pulls in the right one for each job.

architecture.compartmentNote: Sensitive areas like banking stay sealed off. They report back only “done,” never the underlying numbers.

privacy.title: Public frame. Private contents.
privacy.body: Neo splits cleanly in two. The machinery is generic and shareable — anyone could run it. The substance is yours alone and stays locked away. It's the same idea as a journal you can buy empty: the book is for everyone, what you write in it is only yours.

columns.head: Shared / open
items: The Neo loop and workflow logic
The page template you're looking at

privacy.meta: This very page is built that way: the layout is a reusable template, and everything you're reading lives in a separate data file. Swap the file, get a new document.

modules.title: Start small. Then scale.
modules.intro: Proposals proves the whole system. Once that loop runs smoothly, the same shape drops onto everything else.

items.name: Proposals
items.desc: Voice to drafted, reviewed, approved proposal. The first loop we build.

items.name: Landlord
items.desc: Tenant messages, maintenance requests, and recurring property admin.

items.name: Air Force web dev
items.desc: Website updates and development tasks, tracked and reviewed before they ship.

items.name: Mental health app
items.desc: Product and content work for the app, run through the same review loop.

items.name: Finance & stocks
items.desc: A sealed-off module — reports status only, never exposes account data.

template.title: Structure and substance, kept apart.
template.intro: This document is also the first proposal template. The design never changes; the words come from a data file. To make the next proposal, we copy the data file and rewrite it.
template.caption: data.json — the part that changes per proposal

items.label: Plan documented
items.detail: Full architecture written up and agreed.

items.label: Jira board seeded
items.detail: NEO-1 epic + NEO-2 (Red Cross example) created.

items.label: Add “In Review” column
items.detail: Quick board setting so the workflow has all four stages.

items.label: Scaffold the repo
items.detail: Lay down the Neo skeleton and the private data stub.

items.label: Build the proposals loop
items.detail: Wire voice → ticket → draft → review → approve end to end.

items.name: Dad
items.note: Former SVP at an IT consulting firm. Speaks the requests, gives the final yes.

items.name: Mariah
items.note: GNC engineer at Umbra. Designs and builds Neo; brings the flight-software discipline.

items.name: Reviewers
items.note: Sign off on drafts. Notified by on-call, ping, or dashboard depending on contract.

footer.credit: Designed & built by Mariah · Arlington, VA · June 2026
